message.content: How can one include variable and function naming rules?

file.content:
version: "2"

run:
  timeout: 5m

formatters:
  enable:
    - gofumpt      # gofumpt formatter

  settings:
    gofumpt:
      module-path: github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend

linters:
  enable:
    - staticcheck  # staticcheck = true
    - unparam      # unusedparams = true
    - nilnil       # nilness = true (catches nil returns of nil interface values)
    - govet        # base vet; nilness + unusedwrite via govet analyzers

  settings:
    govet:
      enable:
        - nilness
        - unusedwrite

exclusions:
  paths:
    - .git
    - .vscode
    - .idea
    - node_modules
  rules:
    - path: _test\.go
      linters:
        - unparam

content.text:
In golangci-lint v2, naming rules are enforced via the **`revive`** or **`stylecheck`** linters. Here are the main options:

---

## Option 1: `revive` (most flexible)

```yaml
linters:
  enable:
    - revive

  settings:
    revive:
      rules:
        - name: var-naming          # enforces Go naming conventions (camelCase, acronyms)
        - name: exported            # exported names must have doc comments
        - name: unexported-return   # unexported types not returned from exported functions
        - name: receiver-naming     # consistent receiver names
        - name: package-comments
```

For custom patterns you can use `revive`'s `var-declaration` and configure specific naming via regex:

```yaml
        - name: var-naming
          arguments:
            - ["ID", "URL", "HTTP"]   # allowed all-caps acronyms
            - ["Err"]                  # allowed prefixes
```

---

## Option 2: `stylecheck` (part of staticcheck suite)

You already have `staticcheck` enabled. `stylecheck` is a separate linter from the same author:

```yaml
linters:
  enable:
    - stylecheck

  settings:
    stylecheck:
      checks: ["all", "-ST1000"]    # ST1003 = naming conventions
      initialisms: ["ACL", "API", "ASCII", "CPU", "CSS", "DNS", "EOF",
                    "GUID", "HTML", "HTTP", "HTTPS", "ID", "IP", "JSON",
                    "QPS", "RAM", "RPC", "SLA", "SMTP", "SQL", "SSH",
                    "TCP", "TLS", "TTL", "UDP", "UI", "GID", "UID", "UUID",
                    "URI", "URL", "UTF8", "VM", "XML", "XMPP", "XSRF", "XSS"]
```

Key `stylecheck` checks:
- `ST1003` — naming conventions (acronyms, camelCase)
- `ST1020` — exported functions must have comments
- `ST1021` — exported types must have comments

---

## Option 3: Custom regex via `gocritic` or `godot`

For truly custom function/variable name patterns (e.g. "all handlers must end in `Handler`"), use **`revive`** with the `var-naming` rule or a custom rule plugin. `golangci-lint` doesn't natively support arbitrary regex naming rules without a plugin.

---

## Recommended addition for this project

Given you already have `staticcheck`, adding `stylecheck` is the lowest-friction option since they share the same engine:

```yaml
linters:
  enable:
    - stylecheck

  settings:
    stylecheck:
      checks: ["ST1003"]   # naming only
```

Or add `revive` for more granular control (receiver naming, unexported returns, etc.).

input.content:
# Plan: Add naming linter to .golangci.yml

## Context
User wants to enforce Go naming conventions (acronym casing, camelCase) in the existing golangci-lint v2 config. The project already uses `staticcheck`; adding `stylecheck` is the lowest-friction option since both share the same engine.

## Change

**File:** `.golangci.yml`

Add `stylecheck` to the `linters.enable` list and configure it to check `ST1003` (naming conventions — acronyms like ID, URL, HTTP must be all-caps, camelCase enforced):

```yaml
linters:
  enable:
    - staticcheck
    - unparam
    - nilnil
    - govet
    - stylecheck      # <-- add

  settings:
    stylecheck:
      checks: ["ST1003"]
    govet:
      ...
```

`ST1003` covers:
- Acronyms must be consistently cased (e.g. `userID` not `userId`, `httpURL` not `httpUrl`)
- Package names must be lowercase, no underscores
- No stutter in exported names (e.g. avoid `http.HTTPClient`)

## Verification

```bash
golangci-lint run
```

Any naming violations will be reported as `stylecheck` findings.

version: "2"

run:
  timeout: 5m

formatters:
  enable:
    - gofumpt      # gofumpt formatter

  settings:
    gofumpt:
      module-path: github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend

linters:
  enable:
    - staticcheck  # staticcheck = true
    - unparam      # unusedparams = true
    - nilnil       # nilness = true (catches nil returns of nil interface values)
    - govet        # base vet; nilness + unusedwrite via govet analyzers

  settings:
    govet:
      enable:
        - nilness
        - unusedwrite

exclusions:
  paths:
    - .git
    - .vscode
    - .idea
    - node_modules
  rules:
    - path: _test\.go
      linters:
        - unparam

lines: - nilnil       # nilness = true (catches nil returns of nil interface values)
- govet        # base vet; nilness + unusedwrite via govet analyzers


Run `golangci-lint run` to see any existing naming violations.

input.description: Check if generated files have do-not-edit markers
Check if tools dir has Go files

content.text:
Here's what I found:

- `internal/graph/generated/generated.go` — generated by gqlgen (`DO NOT EDIT`)
- `internal/api/docs.go` — generated by swaggo (`DO NOT EDIT`)
- `node_modules` is already listed but lives under `web/frontend/node_modules` (irrelevant since golangci only processes `.go` files)
- `tools/` contains real Go code that should still be linted
- `.git`, `.vscode`, `.idea` already covered

content.content: File unchanged since last read. The content from the earlier Read tool_result in this conversation is still current — refer to that instead of re-reading.

version: "2"

run:
  timeout: 5m

formatters:
  enable:
    - gofumpt # gofumpt formatter

  settings:
    gofumpt:
      module-path: github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend

linters:
  enable:
    - staticcheck # staticcheck = true
    - unparam # unusedparams = true
    - nilnil # nilness = true (catches nil returns of nil interface values)
    - govet # base vet; nilness + unusedwrite via govet analyzers
    - stylecheck # naming conventions (ST1003: acronyms, camelCase)

  settings:
    stylecheck:
      checks: ["ST1003"]

    govet:
      enable:
        - nilness
        - unusedwrite

exclusions:
  paths:
    - .git
    - .vscode
    - .idea
    - node_modules
  rules:
    - path: _test\.go
      linters:
        - unparam

lines: +    - _test\.go                 # test files excluded from all linters

content.text:
Changes made:
- Added `internal/graph/generated` — gqlgen-generated GraphQL code
- Added `internal/api/docs\.go` — swaggo-generated Swagger docs
- Moved `_test\.go` from a per-linter rule to the global `paths` exclusion, so **all** linters are skipped for test files (previously only `unparam` was excluded)
